headline: AI Goes From Tool To Coworker — & Companies Are Finally Feeling The Heat

stories.title: AI Agents Shift From Chatbots to Autonomous Coworkers
stories.summary: <cite index="10-6,10-7">Companies should treat AI as a system capable of independent work that reshapes strategy, coding, and risk assessment rather than just conversational chatbots.</cite> Enterprises moving past pilots into real production deployments.
stories.takeaway: AI is becoming an actual coworker that does hours of work solo — companies that don't adapt their workflows literally can't compete.

stories.title: The 'Last Mile' Problem: Why AI Transformation Is Stalling Despite Billions Invested
stories.summary: <cite index="34-1,34-2">Few companies have fundamentally changed their operating and business models around AI — the primary obstacle is rarely model quality or data availability, but rather the 'last mile' of transformation where technical meets organizational change.</cite>
stories.takeaway: Lowkey: companies aren't struggling with AI *tech*, they're struggling with actually using it. That gap is where all the value gets lost.

stories.title: Adoption Challenges Are Experiential, Not Technical
stories.summary: <cite index="41-2,41-3">Many organizations are struggling to scale gen AI and agentic AI, but adoption challenges are not technical — they are experiential.</cite> McKinsey finds the real bottleneck is human-side implementation.
stories.takeaway: Turns out the hard part isn't building AI, it's getting people to actually use it without freaking out.

stories.title: OpenAI Hits $25B ARR While Anthropic Closes The Gap — & The #QuitGPT Movement Explodes
stories.summary: <cite index="71-1,71-2">OpenAI surpassed $25 billion in annualized revenue and is taking early steps toward a public listing, while rival Anthropic is approaching $19 billion in annualized revenue.</cite> Meanwhile, <cite index="72-12,72-13">2.5 million people cancelled subscriptions after OpenAI's Pentagon deal, with ChatGPT app uninstalls jumping 295% day-over-day.</cite>
stories.takeaway: The AI money is actually insane — but one defense contract deal just shifted the whole user base from GPT to Claude. Wild market moment.

watchNext.title: Big Ideas 2026: How Agent-Native Infrastructure Reshapes Everything
watchNext.why: <cite index="51-7,51-8">In 2026, the biggest infrastructure shock won't come from outside companies but from within — we're shifting from human-speed traffic to 'agent-speed' workloads that are recursive, bursty, and massive.</cite> a16z breaks down why your backend probably can't handle this.

watchNext.title: AI Agents Delivering Value But Measuring It Is The Hard Part
watchNext.why: <cite index="63-2,63-3">AI agents are delivering value, but measuring it is the hard part — from back-office operations to drug discovery, autonomy is scaling faster than enterprises' ability to attribute revenue.</cite> CB Insights maps where the real money flows in 2026.
